title: OOF calibration-aware stacking with rank-preserving calibrators
summary: This is an AUC task, so calibration should not destroy ranking.
rationale: This is an AUC task, so calibration should not destroy ranking. Scikit-learn notes sigmoid calibration preserves ranking while isotonic can introduce ties; beta calibration often handles skewed score distortions better than Platt alone. That makes fold-safe calibrated stacking a practical ensemble path without changing core feature logic.
implementation_hint: Generate out-of-fold predictions from 2-3 diverse models such as CatBoost, LightGBM/XGBoost, and a sequence-aware model. Fit calibrators on OOF scores only: try sigmoid or beta calibration per model globally, and isotonic only inside broad high-sample regimes like dry vs wet or early vs late race. Then train a simple logistic stacker on calibrated OOF predictions.
expected_effect: Small but reliable lift from complementary error patterns, with less leaderboard volatility than raw averaging.
risk: Calibration can reduce AUC if isotonic creates many ties or if regime buckets are too small; prefer rank-preserving calibrators first and require gains on grouped OOF AUC, not only log loss.
